What I'm about to show you is a secret of how I was able to open six gyms off profit in three years as a 23 to 26 year old. And oh, where's my little clicker? Somebody walk my clicker? Somebody take my clicker. All right, well, if someone can find clicker, that'd be cool. All right, and become apparently unbeknownst to me, the biggest gym guru by revenue within less than a year. And challenge very, very, very long standing incumbents in this space. And I'm 1,000% not saying this to both of them. I'm saying how crazy is that? And it's by using the exact same process we did to build and scale the gyms and using the exact same thing in this business. And so if you're like, man, I wish I could just have a breakthrough, it could happen in a year. It could happen in six months and you could have all of your wildest dreams. So if there is a secret to my slash our tremendous ability to grow with the speed, this is the thing. So would anyone like to know what it is? Yes, good, they want to know. All right, cool. So I'll explain in a second. And for now, let me explain my objectives and I'll circle back. That is called an open loop. So my objectives here is to increase your conviction so that you should all be selling at 599 by now and most should be considering the refundless challenge. Okay, that is my goal. So by the end of this presentation, you should feel fine selling at $600 and that you are okay selling a refundless challenge. That's my goal. So all of your refund rates go away, which means that you're automatically adding another 10% to the revenue and then end the headache and you're adding another 100. So I'm adding $150 per sale by the end of this presentation. Cool, cool. Second, help you scale your skill. So the money you make from showing other people how to do things better than you and the amount of impact, the amount of money that you're able to make is never usually directly from what you're able to do but what you're able to get someone else to do as well or better than you. I don't sell any of this stuff here. And what you guys have been able to do, man, it sounds so tooting, honing, horny, but like... Anyways, so you guys went through a sales training that was 45 minutes long and a lot of you guys, within two or three times of watching that, your closing percentage has doubled. That's an immediate effect. That's a product. That's something that you buy, you put someone through it and something gets better. That's the goal here. And so the more that you can do that with your individual skill sets, the more money you'll be able to make. The more you can transfer skills from yourself to other people, the more money you will be able to make. That's how you leverage. Three, I'm going to help you break beliefs around how to get sales. Four, understand that 80% of my goal, sorry, that 80% is my goal for all of you for closed sales. I broke down the numbers of selling, which I think some of you guys hope... Has anyone seen that video? I'm breaking down the numbers of selling. I'll give you a quick viewpoint. You got 100 people walking the door, 30 are going to be lay down sales. They walked in already ready to buy that includes the six-week challenge people. They never go to the gym. They came in ready to sign up for whatever that program was. 50 people will say yes if you overcome some of their concerns and objections. And then 20 people, the last 20, so we got 30, 50, and then 80, sorry, and then the last 20. They're never going to say yes ever because they're crazy people and that's okay. And they like saying no to feel like they win and they validate themselves and it's awesome. So my goal for you is to close 80% of people who walk in the door for the challenge and you can do it, I promise. When I talk about that story, it's a big part of it. And number five, protect your business with superior closing percentage and query graph sales process. So Kirk, I'm only picking on you because I love you. So Kirk, right now, Kirk has one of the... Like if you look at the five core functions, Kirk's lead generation, lead nurture is phenomenal. Homie crushes it. He gets more people in the door than anyone. It's really like lower closing percentage. I'm saying this with love. If you're in this situation, I'm also saying because I know there's a lot of other people but you have courage and you're great. So if I can get Kirk's closing percentage from 30% to 80%, I can 2.5X his business with no other change besides the words he says and how he says them. Crazy, right? That's how valuable selling is. And that means that if I can 2.5X the revenue from the campaign, then I can 2.5X the spend that I'm spending, which means that I can outspend and protect myself from any competitor who ever wants to come in. I can use more platforms. I can use direct mail. I can use whatever because I know I'm closing every person who walks in the door. That makes sense. Okay. So this is something to give your sales team. Closer formula. All right? Give them all this. It's my first acronym ever. I'm really excited about it. So if you're thinking about them and you tell them this, like, this is something that you can think about. Closer. First thing you do is you clarify why they opted in and showed up. Clarify why they're there. Second, you're going to label them with the problem that you plan to solve. Hey, how's it going? Why'd you sign up? I need to lose weight. Awesome. Three. So what have you done so far to try and lose that weight? Have you done Jenny Craig? You done any other gyms? Go into past pains. Yes. Okay. So you've done all these other things that filled you in the past? Well, I think you might be a good fit for a pro. Can I tell you about it? Yes. Sell them in vacation. And they're like, well, I'm not sure if I can make a fit in my schedule. I have to talk to my husband. I'm not sure it's a lot of money. I've never spent this much money before. Blah, blah, blah, whatever. Right? And then last one, reinforce their decision. So now after they have bought, reinforce the decision. They're going to sign the paperwork and then start them on a keep them hot sequence or whatever you're going to do to make sure that they don't back out. That's the closer formula. Cool? You can tell that to your team. I thought it was nifty. Okay. So let's put the presentation into two pieces. Sales theory and sales tactics. So here's my moral disclaimer. And I'm like, I'm super legit on this. You can sell anything once. Selling to people again requires exceptional service, experience, connection, and results. Otherwise, this will hurt you more than it will help you because the better you are at selling, the more people you'll be able to get to buy your stuff, which means the more you have to fulfill on it. So there's like this, this, the oh shit moment of like, oh my gosh, now I have to fulfill everything I just sold them. And so that's why when Dave here was like, just make sure that your program is freaking amazing. You can sell your ass off. Right? Like we try so hard on the legacy side to make this unbelievable for everyone. And I hope you guys feel that way too, that like I don't feel bad doing everything we can to get people into it because I know it's going to help. Right? So that's going to make you more money. So get that right first and then you'll be able to sell with reckless abandon. If you ever have feelings where you're like, I'm just not sure if we're good, like that will get in the way of your selling before it'll set you will self sabotage because you are a moral person. Like most of us came in this to help people. And if you feel like on any level that you're not helping people, you will sabotage yourself in the selling process because you will feel like you're doing them on a subconscious level a service by not signing them up. And so you're only going to sign up people that like pretty much lay down because you're like, well, okay. I'll take your money. But if someone gives you a couple of concerns, you're like, I'm not going to fight for it because like they might be right. Right? Because a sale always happens. Either you sell them your conviction or they sell you their excuse. Always. If you believe more, you'll win. So part one, sales theory. Why to sell expensive stuff, aka high ticket? This is particularly related to HTTP. Right? If for those of you who are new to the selling world of what we are in, 600, 700 like Joe sells his, you sell yours for 700, right? 699. Right? You sell his challenge for 699. You can do that too. Same close rate. Right? Exactly same close rate. Want to make twice as much money and do nothing different? So cool. Okay. And part of that is because it allows you to outspend everyone and out result everyone else in your marketplace. So this is Tony Robbins. If you want to take the island and you burn your boats, absolute commitment with come the insights that create real victory. And so if you think about this, even in our world, everyone, when they sign off for gym launch, who here had an oh shit moment when they sign off for gym launch for the first time? Right? We burned your boats for you. We were like, you have to do it. Or you're going to go broke. Right? Like I'm going to take your gym. But we did that because I genuinely care. I really do. I really, really, really, really do want you to crush it. And that means that if you feel like you're backs against the wall, then you'll call the leads. You know what I mean? And then when they come in and you have five people in a row who say no, you'll get up tomorrow, call them again and sell again today. Right? Like if you don't have your back against the wall and it's a thousand dollar program, you be like, it was too hard. People are broke. It didn't work. Right? How easy would that have been if it was a $500 commitment? Easy. Right? At this price point, it's not. And so if you think about it the same way with regards to the high ticket sales process for HTP, how much more likely is it that person? Right? I guess I just covered all this stuff. So I'm going to try and stay on track. So we force people to burn their boats. We burn their boats for them. So like if someone's broke, remember this is like another selling trick. If someone is broke, then they are easier to sell. You're easier to sell because they have no mindset around money. Money just flows away from them. They get it, burn it out. Get it, they burn it out. Right? Some of you guys are like this. Don't be that way. But broke people are easier to sell. Rich people are easier to sell too because they have the money. So you always win. Always have that mindset. All right? And if anything, if someone is wealthy, they should pay more just to have the same amount of value in that same program. So a cajillionaire paying $2,000 for a program is kind of irrelevant to them. Right? Maybe they should pay more. Maybe they should charge them more. All right? But you're doing your clients a disservice by not charging them more for a couple of reasons. If we didn't charge what we charge, I couldn't give you mouth. I couldn't give you our entire support team. They would all be in Pakistan, not be able to speak English. Right? And then everyone, they hate us and then it would suck. Right? So not only would you hate me, you'd also get worse results. So it's like, it's a win-win. Everyone's happy or everyone's upset. And so when you have the, you know, if you're paying $89 a month for a boot camp, right, you have no margin left over to do anything cool for your clients, right, or improve anything and you hate your life because you have no margin. And so you have this vicious cycle of they're complaining that they didn't get a meal plan for $89 and you're like, what? Like, you can't even walk into my office for $89. You already wasted my $89 and I wasted my time. So it's just this vicious cycle where on the flip side you have Lauren and Dave's clients who they love to death and go out to barbecues with and like have become great lifelong friends with the HCP clients because like these people value them, they value their time and they value and respect them back. This is beautiful symbiotic relationship. So unpacking the box. We want to help people, right? So we want to be able to want everyone to be able to afford our program. Correct? We want to help as many people as we can. Isn't that the deal? Right? But anyone can afford whatever they want. Within reason, right? By lowering the price, we value ourselves. We tell our customers what they bought isn't that good. And by extension, that they are not worth more. Like that needs to sink in. Like if you sell your service for not a lot of money, you are telling them that they are not worth more. And when you do it that way, you can help less people and you make less impact because you can't afford to market or get anyone. If we didn't market and it had no way of acquiring customers, I wouldn't be able to help anybody here with their gym. For real, right? So when someone gives you 25% of their income, like the Chipotle girl did, right? Probably, maybe half of her income, whatever. One of the most dedicated clients I had drove a beat up piece, like different color patches on his Hyundai, like the black hub cap. I don't know what those are, but it had two of them. And he rolled up and I was like, this is not going to be good. Paid us $500 a month for three years. And I was like, what do you do? He's like, I just work at a warehouse. And I was like, cool, man. Like, you like it? And he's like, here at home, man. He was a spinnaker. But he loved it. He was like, I lost 130 pounds. He's like, this is my place. This is my family. Like he had, like this had become home for him, right? And he had no money. But he paid every month. So it's like, we cut ourselves short when we think like someone doesn't deserve better service, right? Like, some of us sell, like someone walks in and they look broke, you don't even sell them. You're like, oh yeah, John's $500. And they're like, yeah, I can't afford it. You're like, cool. Like, some of you have done that. I know you have. You've seen someone be like, it's not worth the time. Very beginning of this, I said, your work works on you more than you work on it. All labor there is profit, right? So you will learn how to sell more by selling to broke people than selling to rich people. So get better at selling. So learn how to sell. Learn it. Be like, I want to be able to go in the hood and sell $5.99 all day long. Like think about it as a skill that you want to invest in. And if you switch how you see selling, you will get better at it because you don't think about it. You're like, you're investing in yourself. You're sharpening the axe, right? And what happens is, everyone here has played, a lot of here have played sports, right? Okay. Some of you did free throws. Some of you guys did softball. Some of you guys did volleyball, whatever. And then you'd serve hundreds of serves a day, or you'd do whatever it was, right? And it was never like, it wasn't like this, and that's just like the end of my last presentation that got cut off, is that your trajectory of experience levels isn't like this. Usually the breakthroughs are like, oh, I need to flick my wrist a little bit because you did it by accident once. It works the exact same way with all skills. We just used to do it physically. You squat and the beginning it sucks. And then all of a sudden, you somehow like one day, ooh, I did something a little different there. I put my hips forward earlier and I got out of the hole, right? It works the exact same way with selling, right? And so if you can teach your staff by saying, I want to give you the number one thing that every single person's ever worked for me, and this is actually like, if you ask anyone who's ever worked for me in sales, they've been like, Alex taught me more about selling than anyone else I've ever met. And so all of them can feed all of their families now because they work for us. And so I had guys who were making 13, 15 an hour who, if you talk to them now, they're like, Alex set me up for the rest of my life like I can sell. And so if you're in a position where you're like, how do I convince someone to work for me? Like, I'm going to teach you how to sell. You will never have this opportunity. Like, you're going to get a PhD in selling. No other job will you be able to have 20 at-bats a day for months on end and sell 500, 600, $2,000 a pack. I'm so excited. But I don't really know anything about it. And I'm going to get to how this works. But my favorite thing in fitness sales, favorite thing in the entire world, this is like the swoosh of sales, in my opinion. Is when a woman would walk in, it's always a woman. So it could be a guy. In this case, it's a woman. So when a woman's walking out the door and turns around, and then it's like half bewildered and it's like, wait, am I going to be working out here? This is a workout program, right? You're like, yes. They were so sold on the vacation. We didn't talk at all about any of the details in the particulars. But she only saw her husband looking at her in the way that he's never looked at her again. She was so sold on the fact that this Kim Kardashian program, she was going to be Kim Kardashian in 12 weeks. So sold on that concept that she forgot to ask anything else because she was like, I'm in. I want that. I want that. So here's how you do it. Selfification not the plane flight. I've said this to every sales team I've ever had. I don't know anyone else who said it besides me, so I'm quoting it. Selfification not the plane flight. You're selling Maui not the way to get there. So you're not selling this. You're not selling TSA. You're not selling your modules, your meal plan, your macros, your workouts, your support team, your voxer texting, your community. You're not selling any of that because that's the plane flight. That sounds like work. I don't want to take my shoes off, take my belt off, go through security. I don't want to do that. It sounds like work. What do I want? I don't want TSA lines airport packing suitcases, taking off shoes, layovers, connecting flights, et cetera. Person farting in front of you. You sell Maui. You're describing Maui like, do you want to go to Maui? Look at the beaches, look at the beautiful palm trees and the waters. It's going to be so blue. It's going to be amazing. Can you think about the wind in your hair when you have the kidney on? How you look and feel? We always sell the same thing. The difference is the level of service. So the way you sell HTTP and as you learn to sell HTTP, how easy is the challenge sales now? Stupid. How easy are the challenge sales now? Stupid. You're like, what are you talking about? The program. You didn't even talk about the program. That's why we're like, finish your shindigale belt. You can work out. Yes, cool. Boom. We're just checking boxes off. That's all we're doing. The difference is the level of service and how they get there. But the outcome is the same. So do they want to swim to Maui? They're sharks. You can be drowned or get robbed. Maui's probably far away. Like that. Aggressive. Do you want to take a boat to Maui? It'll take a while. They're slow, slash no Wi-Fi, which is always my concern. They're going to be in the sun, get burned, all that stuff. Or do they want to take a normal flight to Maui? Just fly commercial. They'll get there faster. It still smells bad. House security, bad food, but they're going to get there. Or do you want to take a private jet to Maui? Direct, nonstop, champagne on the plane, suitcase pack when they arrive. Everyone gets to Maui. This is what a lot of people super mess up. Especially if you have multiple offers. If you want a price anchor high, like if we have HTTP and you're doing it internally, you have a referral who walks in the door, price anchor high, and then you can down sell. The variables are the speed and the quality of the journey. Everyone's going to lose weight. You can't say, like with integrity, you can't say, well, we have this other program, you won't really lose that much weight on it. You can't say that. So the question is the speed and the quality of the journey. Now, like I said, the likelihood of arrival, though, is assumed because it is a sale. So what you sell is always the same thing. Multiple levels of service in the business, but we sell the exact same thing. We're going to fill up your jet for us and help you make more money. That's what we do. So what vehicle do you want? How long do you want it to take you? How much support do you want along the way? Everything's the same here with weight loss. Would you feel more comfortable in a large group or do you want to work out with me individually? You can say that, but it's in a small group, but whatever. How much support would you feel more comfortable with? And usually you can pitch a small group more easily because you can say, would you rather, everyone would rather do the small group, virtually. Who's going to say no to that? And so if they say that first, they'll be far more likely to actually buy that if you present that first. A lot of people are really afraid of saying prices. Don't. People won't run away. They're like, okay, that's not in my budget. Well, cool. Well, how's $6.99? I can probably do it. We can split into three. Okay, I'm in. So you can downsell them. As long as you have good rapport in the sale, don't be afraid of it. They're not going to be offended. They're not going to flip the table over. They'll be fine. And again, the sale, as you learn how to sell high ticket and you get acquainted with that really, really selling, the 6-week challenge will feel really easy. It'll feel like no money. And part of it is your attitude around money will change. I'm just telling you. Well, because you're just going to say it like it's not even that big of a deal. And they will mirror that and they will stop feeling like it's a big deal. Which it's hard because a lot of the people that I start working with are really broke. And so like, they're like, it's $500. I know you don't have the money. I don't either. You know what I mean? Like, and so they said, I'm sorry. You can start for $99. Like the first month's free. Like you don't even need to give my credit card. Just come back later. You know what I mean? Like, so, but they, so it's like one of the hardest things. Is to get someone out of scarcity into abundance. It's the weirdest thing because once you have money, money flows, it's the like, it's. It's the weirdest thing. But all of these sales revolve around the singular concept of a promise. Okay. All of sales. One promise. Okay. And the promise is always the same. Okay. This is like huge. Which is why I think sales ability is actually a commodity. So as someone who is proclaimed and other people say that I'm some selling expert, I think that I think ability to sell is really a commodity. I don't, I don't value the ability that much. I think that you can train someone on it fairly easily, especially if you have the right process in place. So the question is, like I said at the beginning, this is like my big secret, right? So how do we sell so much more than every other coaching guru in our space? How do we do that? This is part two. Sales tactics. Because we understand two basic questions that need to be answered. Okay. Within the process, within the process of selling, especially high ticket. Who wants to know what they are? Okay. Cool. I just want to see if I got you guys engaged or just like it doesn't off. All right. So quarterstone question number one. If XYZ does what I say, or what we say it does, is it worth what we're asking for it? Some of you guys have probably heard me say this to you on the phone. Like, if legacy does what I say it does, is it worth what I'm charging? Most people say yes. B2B selling, it's easier because you have money to money exchange. But the second part of the, it's, there's a flip flop in terms of how easy it is on emotional selling. So what we're doing when we're selling weight loss is emotional sales. What we're doing when we're selling B2B is, is a business sale, which is usually comes around money. So, but if I, if like, if we can get you, right, and this is where you build value. So the easiest way to build value is ask them what you want to have happen. Right. That's in the beginning of the sale. Clarify why they're there. What do you want to have happen? 12 weeks from now, what would make you ecstatically happy? What would make you the happiest woman on earth? Like, don't just ask like, what would make you satisfied? Be like, what would make you so pumped that you couldn't stop telling your friends about how awesome we are and how great this experience was. Right. Sorry. Okay. Good. Um, and we like to find emotional anchors for behavior change because it makes them six times more likely to stick. Do you mind if I ask you a few candid questions about that outcome? Right. That's the question. If you like, need to have a good question to ask someone about, like for high ticket, we like to find emotional anchors for behavior age because it makes you more likely six times more likely to stick to this program. And I'm asking you, mind if I ask you a few more candid questions? So I just got permission to ask them whatever I want to ask. Right. And so we can get like what Dom was saying, like everybody says that they want to get healthy, but like, what do you really want? You know what I mean? Like what's going to, what's going to keep you up at night. Right. And so when you ask them those types of questions and you have the right pre frame, what do you want to have happen when you're building value? The only thing they have to do after that is just repeat back to them. What they just said to you. That's it. I would really like, I just really want to have more time, you know, with my family and I just want the gym to run kind of like on autopilot damaging admission. I would respond to it. Be like, it's never going to run on autopilot. But what I can tell you, and then they believe whatever the next thing is, right? Same thing here. So if someone's trying to lose weight, you're like, listen, is it like, do I not like donuts? I still like donuts, but, but I can't tell you using our program, you'll be able to eat donuts and you'll never have to cut them out for life. And you'll still be able to look the way you want to look. That's not fair. You just want to believe them all the time. That okay? Yes, that's okay. Damaging admission. Now I believe you. Right. Stuff making sense. This cool. Okay. So when you're repeating back to them, what they just said, you're doing it with enhanced color. So you're not just going to say the words, you're going to say it like poetry, right? You're going to keep enhancing and repackaging it until it become ridiculous for them to say, no, and this is where you're getting the first agreement. So there's two questions that we need to get on the promise, right? For them to agree to. First one was if it does what I say it does, is it worth what I'm charging? This is what we're talking about now. So you're telling me that if you live for 10 more years, I got this from Jeff Katz, um, where you at? Here you go. If you're, so you're telling me that if you live for 10 more years, you never felt tired again. You never had brain fogging in your children lived healthy, active lives by example, and all their children lived 10 years longer. That wouldn't be worth three grand. And they're like, well, I mean, it would be what, so like, so I have agreement. So it's like, you're telling me I got a chance, right? Like, so you're in that point in the sales or you're like, yeah, so you were telling me if we did all this for you, like it wouldn't be worth 29 97. They're like, no, I'd be worth that. I'd like, okay, cool. Boom. Now we have agreement. And I like, now we're going to the question number two. Cause that now mind you, this is why you better be darn sure that you're good at what you do. Right. So you can sell like this, right? Humor. Okay. Sales note. So this is the side note. Like with emotional services, what we're talking about B to C agreement one is more difficult than agreement two. So that first one that I just talked about is harder than the second agreement. I'm going to talk to you about in a second with B to B number one is easier. Number two is harder. It's easier to say, well, if I make you 10 grand, is it worth? Or if I make you a hundred, will you pay me 10 for it? Yes. It's an easy agreement. If you can do that, it's true with B to B. It's harder to do the second one, which is. All right. Well, I'll just continue my presentation. I'll get the second question, which, which is why claims on the first question get bigger and bigger and more outlandish because marketers and business owners think they need to promise harder. That's the belief. I actually need to promise bigger, like seven-figure gym owners, eight-figure gym owners, nine-figure mastermind, whatever, right? Like we just have to promise bigger. I'm poking. That's okay. Anyways, so, which is why claims on the first question get bigger and bigger and more outlandish because they feel like they need to promise harder, but that's not the case. They're forgetting the second half of the sales equation, right? Because once you've made a promise and they agree that the price is fair, like I said, it's easier in B to B than B to C, then the only thing you have left is whether they believe you. That's it. So like if you believe me and I said that if you give me 10 grand, I can make you a hundred, go buy. If you believe me, right? So you asked the second question, which I'll get to later. Cool? Okay. So what would I need to do or what would you need to see in order to believe that this is something that can happen for you, right? Using our program. Like what would you need to do or see? Have you guys noticed with gym launch, the way the sales process works? We don't hard pitch you. What would you need to see? How many gym owners we need to talk to? How many posts inside of that growth do you need to see before you start believing? Right? And the beauty of that question is it's not a yes or no answer. Right? And so someone can't say, so we said, if this does what I say it does, is it worth X amount of money? Yes. Okay. What would you need to do to believe me? There's no yes or no. They cannot deny. They can't say it's too expensive. I didn't ask them yet. Right? They can't, they can't say it's too expensive because they said, yes, it would be worth that. And I'm like, how would you believe me? And then they tell me what they would need to believe me or for me, you go to the same, right? And once they tell you what you need to see, I have to show it to them, which is why we have pages of testimonials, walls, books, all of these things. So you're like, do you need a hundred? I need 200. And then at what point is it more unreasonable for you to not believe me than for you to believe me? Right? How many gym owners you need to see? Right? Again, and you're doing this to the point of ridiculousness. That's the idea. Right? So how do you make someone believe? And so to answer this question, we looked at our favorite and best buyers. And this is something that we, this is, this is when we had a big quantum leap in how gym launch got sold. Right? And what we looked at was this is when we changed the, the selling process. This is when Layla and I were still selling in the beginning. It was like the sales were too hard. Like you can feel when sales aren't right. You're like, it's like, there's too much fighting. You know what I mean? There's like, I'm really like strong already in this. I don't, I don't like the feel of it. Right? And so I was like, how can I make this better? And so I looked at all of my favorite people to work with. And I was like, all right, look at these people. And what was their experience? Right? And so then we reverse engineered them. I was like, so what's the ideal client look like? So the ideal client for us, right? I'm talking about gym launch. This client has seen all of the posts in our free group. And look, they've scrolled on it forever. Right? They've seen hundreds of testimonials. They have 10 best friends who did the program. They have bodies. I said, this is now this is giving you a weight loss example, but for the gym owner, it'd be the same thing. So, you know, it's 10 best friends who, who attend other CrossFit gyms who have done the exact same program with business models that are identical. There's who all had crazy results and fill the gyms up in 30 days. And they all told her or him, you're an idiot if you don't sign up. Right? How hard does that person sell? Not hard. Right? Because especially HTTP people, they got rich friends. And their rich friends need to lose weight too. OK. OK. So mind you, all of this selling stuff hinges on your ability to produce results that you promise consistently for people in identical circumstances as them and maintain providing value. And so what happens when you do all this? When you start the conversation, you have this process where they opt in. They get a text message. They see all these people who have transformed. And then they go into a group. And they have all these other people who have transformed being like, you need to do it. It's amazing. Oh my god, it changed my life. And then they walk into this guy who's been edified as a freaking deity. And this whole time, what happens, right? You've actually engineered a organic buying experience. Most of our clients, I'll say most of, a lot of people have said, I heard about you from a friend. Or I stumbled on you. They're like, I didn't even know they saw an ad. And that's actually not from Dan Kennedy. That's a paraphrasing what Dan Kennedy says. So Dan Kennedy says, you can spend the most to a product customer wins. This is Alex's paraphrasing. A business beats its competition by making the same prospect worth more to his business than that of its competition. I feel like it's said a little bit more clearly. So for example, we're competing with, we're not just competing with Weight Watchers. So if we have a 40, we have a 40-year-old woman. We're thinking, we're competing against Weight Watchers, P90X, whatever, right? We're actually competing against her hair salon. We're competing against her dry cleaner. We're competing against kids' daycare that's trying to market to her. We're competing against clothing. We're competing against all of the things that are trying to buy her attention. I compete against rubber manufacturers, equipment dealers. All those people are people who are trying to buy Jim owner's attention, right? But if I can provide more value to Jim owners than a rubber manufacturer and equipment dealer, then I'll be able to spend more than they will to acquire them, and then I'll be able to get them, right? Same thing here. We're able to make more money, hopefully, than a hairstylist can, than buying clothes can. And so these are all the people that we're buying attention against. And so if we can do that, you no longer need to find your ideal prospect, but you'll actually create them. Cool? And nifty? Okay, and so when you can reframe someone in such a way that they actually come to speak with you, they're already excited. They've already consumed your content. They've spoken to 10 of your current customers. They have three people in their exact same position that they had the transformation that they're looking for. The only question that they have for you is, how do I get started? Cool? And then you change your customers' lives. And that's when you transform industries overnight and change the world. And for some reason, that went out of order. So it's supposed to say, just a word of your customers. But let's put it there. That's my sales presentation. Cool? Thank you.
